dataCategories.name: Account data
dataCategories.description: Account numbers, balances, statements, and transaction histories.
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: Includes current account, card account, and custody account records.
deidentification_requirements: Aggregate or hash account identifiers for analytics
cross_border_constraints: Bank secrecy overlays may restrict transfers

dataCategories.name: Biometric auth data
dataCategories.description: Facial, voice, and behavioral biometrics for authentication.
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: Template storage and replay risks require enhanced safeguards.
cross_border_constraints: Explicit consent and local labor law constraints may apply

dataCategories.name: Payment card data
dataCategories.description: PAN, CVV, track data, and PIN-related elements.
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: Any system storing, processing, or transmitting PAN is in scope.
deidentification_requirements: Do not store CVV after authorization

dataCategories.name: Credit data
dataCategories.description: Credit history, bureau scores, and underwriting data.
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: Includes AI-driven decision features and adverse action rationale.
deidentification_requirements: Bias testing data should be pseudonymized where possible
cross_border_constraints: Automated decision transparency duties vary by jurisdiction

dataCategories.name: Digital asset data
dataCategories.description: Wallet addresses, custody keys metadata, and ledger transactions.
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: Private keys are treated as critical secrets, not personal data alone.
deidentification_requirements: On-chain analytics should limit direct identity linkage
cross_border_constraints: Travel Rule applies for qualifying transfers

dataCategories.name: Insurance data
dataCategories.description: Policy, underwriting, claims, and actuarial datasets.
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: May include special-category personal data in health lines.
deidentification_requirements: Claims analytics should de-identify beneficiaries when feasible
cross_border_constraints: Reinsurance transfer requires contract and privacy controls

dataCategories.name: KYC/AML data
dataCategories.description: Identity evidence, beneficial ownership, sanctions and PEP screening information.
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: Includes verification artifacts and monitoring outcomes.
cross_border_constraints: Travel rule obligations for virtual asset service providers

dataCategories.name: NPI
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: Includes personally identifiable customer financial information and inferences.
cross_border_constraints: SCCs for EU to third-country transfers

dataCategories.name: Open banking data
dataCategories.description: API account/transaction data and consent records for AIS/PIS.
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: Includes customer consents, token scopes, and TPP registration details.
deidentification_requirements: Remove customer identifiers for product analytics
cross_border_constraints: Cross-border TPP calls require contractual and consent safeguards

dataCategories.name: SWIFT messaging
dataCategories.description: MT/MX payment and correspondent banking messages.
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: Includes FIN, FileAct, and message metadata retained for screening.
deidentification_requirements: Sanctions and AML records must preserve forensic traceability
cross_border_constraints: Cross-border correspondent chains require sanctions and AML checks

dataCategories.name: Trading data
dataCategories.description: Order flow, positions, execution and market data records.
dataCategories.boundary_conditions: Includes algo parameters where they influence client execution.
cross_border_constraints: Local market abuse reporting obligations persist

threatScenarios.name: Account takeover at scale
threatScenarios.description: Credential stuffing and session hijack campaigns on digital banking portals.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Adversaries automate login attempts, bypass weak MFA enrollment, and trigger payout fraud before anti-fraud controls activate.

threatScenarios.name: Transaction monitoring rule evasion
threatScenarios.description: Structuring and synthetic identity tactics to avoid AML controls.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Bad actors split transfers and manipulate onboarding information to remain below alert thresholds.
historical_incidents: Regulatory enforcement for ineffective AML monitoring

threatScenarios.name: Cloud control plane compromise in financial workloads
threatScenarios.description: Compromise of cloud identity/control plane leads to broad disruption or unauthorized access across core financial services.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Adversary obtains high-privilege cloud credentials, alters network/security policy, and disrupts or exfiltrates multi-service financial workloads.
detection_indicators: privilege escalation in cloud IAM root/organization accounts
security group or firewall policy drift on critical services
cross-region service outage correlated to unauthorized config changes
historical_incidents: Large-scale cloud IAM and control plane incidents affecting financial service availability

threatScenarios.name: Card-not-present fraud
threatScenarios.description: Fraudulent remote card transactions exploiting weak controls in ecommerce payment flows.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Attackers test card lists and abuse poor challenge rules to authorize stolen cards.
historical_incidents: Large-scale CNP fraud incidents in ecommerce

threatScenarios.name: Core ledger manipulation
threatScenarios.description: Unauthorized balance or posting changes in core banking systems.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Attacker gains privileged access to posting APIs, submits fraudulent reversal and adjustment entries, then suppresses reconciliation alerts.
historical_incidents: Financial statement control failures involving privileged posting abuse

threatScenarios.name: Hot wallet key extraction
threatScenarios.description: Compromise of online custody keys enabling unauthorized transfer of digital assets.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Attackers exploit wallet infrastructure to access signing material and drain custodial accounts.

threatScenarios.name: Authorized push payment fraud at instant rails
threatScenarios.description: Social-engineering and mule-account techniques used to induce real-time irrevocable payments to attacker-controlled beneficiaries.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Attackers compromise trust channels, manipulate payee confidence checks, and trigger high-velocity transfers before fraud controls escalate.
detection_indicators: new-payee transfer bursts from mature accounts
session device mismatch with first-time beneficiary
sudden outbound velocity after account profile change
historical_incidents: Escalation of APP and mule-account fraud across instant payment programs

threatScenarios.name: Market data feed poisoning
threatScenarios.description: Tampering with market data inputs used by trading engines.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Compromised feed handlers inject manipulated pricing signals to influence automated execution.

threatScenarios.name: Model poisoning and drift in credit decisioning
threatScenarios.description: Manipulated or degraded model inputs/feedback loops distort credit decisions and fairness outcomes.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Attacker injects crafted application attributes and feedback artifacts, causing model drift and potentially discriminatory lending outcomes.
detection_indicators: sudden feature-distribution divergence in accepted vs rejected applications
unexpected credit loss increase by protected-class proxy segments
historical_incidents: Regulatory focus on explainability and fairness breakdowns in digital lending models

threatScenarios.name: TPP impersonation
threatScenarios.description: Fraudulent third-party provider credentials used to access AIS/PIS APIs.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Attacker forges or steals TPP credentials and manipulates consent scope for unauthorized access.
historical_incidents: API ecosystem impersonation incidents in fintech integrations

threatScenarios.name: Regulatory reporting manipulation
threatScenarios.description: Intentional or accidental tampering of supervisory submissions.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Adversary modifies XBRL pipeline transformations or suppresses reconciliation errors before filing.

threatScenarios.name: Sanctions screening evasion via nested counterparties
threatScenarios.description: Adversaries hide sanctioned ownership or payment origin through nested entities and routing intermediaries.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Attackers fragment counterparties and transaction attributes to evade static screening rules and exploit weak ongoing due diligence.
detection_indicators: repeated near-match sanctions hits with rapid beneficiary changes
counterparty ownership changes shortly before high-risk transfers
nested correspondent routing with inconsistent originator data
historical_incidents: Enforcement actions tied to weak sanctions and correspondent banking controls

threatScenarios.name: SCA bypass and downgrade attack
threatScenarios.description: Manipulation of payment auth flow to bypass strong customer authentication.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Attacker forces fallback methods and exploits weak challenge routing to execute unauthorized payments.

threatScenarios.name: SWIFT operator credential theft
threatScenarios.description: Compromise of SWIFT Alliance operator credentials leading to fraudulent payment instruction.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Adversary compromises operator endpoint and message workflows to submit unauthorized MT messages with delayed detection.

threatScenarios.name: Trading algorithm parameter tampering
threatScenarios.description: Unauthorized changes to strategy parameters resulting in market abuse or losses.
threatScenarios.attack_narrative: Insider or attacker modifies order limits/latency controls to trigger manipulative behavior.

technicalStandards.name: Berlin Group NextGenPSD2
technicalStandards.scope: EU PSD2 API profile for AIS/PIS
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Bind consent to customer auth and certificate identity, and monitor TPP scope usage.
technicalStandards.licensing_restrictions: Use profile summaries and references only.

technicalStandards.name: CPMI-IOSCO Cyber Guidance for FMIs
technicalStandards.scope: Cyber resilience expectations for payment, clearing, and settlement infrastructures
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Define impact tolerances, sector-wide scenario testing, and time-bound recovery objectives for critical payment services.

technicalStandards.name: 3DS 2.x / EMV 3DS
technicalStandards.scope: Strong customer authentication for card-not-present flows
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Use risk-based authentication with strict fallback controls.

technicalStandards.name: OpenID FAPI 2.0 Security Profile
technicalStandards.scope: High-assurance API security profile for financial-grade open banking interfaces
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Use sender-constrained access tokens, signed requests/responses, and deterministic consent/transaction binding.

technicalStandards.name: FDX API
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Enforce least-privilege scopes and consent lifecycle tracking.

technicalStandards.name: FIX Protocol
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Enforce signed sessions and strict sequence/duplication handling.

technicalStandards.name: ISO 20022
technicalStandards.scope: Financial messaging data model and message definitions
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Apply schema validation and message signing controls at ingress and egress.
technicalStandards.licensing_restrictions: Standard text subject to ISO licensing.

technicalStandards.name: ISO 27017/27018
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Apply cloud-native access boundaries and processor assurance evidence.

technicalStandards.name: NIST SP 800-86
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Align fraud and breach investigations with forensic chain-of-custody.

technicalStandards.name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
technicalStandards.scope: Cybersecurity governance and outcome-based risk management for financial institutions
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Translate CSF outcomes into institution-level KRIs/KPIs and board reporting aligned to supervisory expectations.

technicalStandards.name: PCI DSS 4.0
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Constrain CDE scope, tokenize PAN, and enforce strong IAM with quarterly testing.
technicalStandards.licensing_restrictions: Public summary and requirements references only.

technicalStandards.name: STIX/TAXII
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Integrate FS-ISAC feeds with detection engineering pipelines.

technicalStandards.name: SWIFT CSP
technicalStandards.scope: SWIFT messaging security controls and attestation
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Segregate operator roles, harden endpoints, and verify release workflow integrity.

technicalStandards.name: UK Open Banking Standard
technicalStandards.scope: UK open banking API and security profile
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Maintain detached signatures and robust consent revocation handling.

technicalStandards.name: XBRL
technicalStandards.implementation_guidance: Use deterministic transformation pipelines and lineage checks.

EU.obligation: Risk-based AML transaction monitoring and sanctions controls with supervisory expectations under AMLD5/AMLD6.
EU.trigger: high-risk customer/activity profile or sanctions-relevant transaction event

US.obligation: Implement BSA/OFAC screening, escalation, and reporting controls for prohibited counterparties and suspicious activity.
US.timeline: real-time or near real-time screening + SAR timelines where applicable
US.trigger: match/near-match sanctions hit, suspicious pattern, or prohibited jurisdiction nexus

EU.obligation: Notify supervisory authority within 72 hours for personal data breaches.
EU.trigger: Likely risk to rights and freedoms

SE.obligation: GDPR breach reporting plus financial resilience incident reporting under DORA when applicable.
SE.trigger: Data breach and significant ICT incident

US-CA.obligation: Notify affected residents for data breach incidents.

US-NY.obligation: Report qualifying cybersecurity events to NYDFS and satisfy state notice requirements.
US-NY.trigger: material cybersecurity event / breach of private information

DORA.obligation: Classify ICT incidents and provide initial/intermediate/final reports to competent authority.

NYDFS.obligation: Notify NYDFS of qualifying cybersecurity event.
NYDFS.trigger: event with notice obligations or material impact

EU.obligation: Apply SCA and transaction risk analysis controls for electronic payment initiation and account access.
EU.timeline: pre-transaction authentication and continuous fraud monitoring
EU.trigger: electronic payment initiation or account access request

US.obligation: Maintain risk-based fraud controls and incident response for real-time payment services handling consumer account data.
US.timeline: pre- and post-transaction controls with rapid case escalation
US.trigger: suspicious new-payee behavior, social-engineering indicators, or anomalous payment velocity

PSD2.obligation: Apply strong customer authentication and secure communication for electronic payments.

REG_E.obligation: Investigate and resolve consumer EFT error claims within statutory windows.
REG_E.timeline: 10 business days (provisional credit extension rules apply)

SCF.EN-01.title: Encryption at rest and in transit

SCF.FD-01.title: Real-time fraud detection and mule interdiction

SCF.TP-02.title: Third-party ICT risk and concentration governance

State-by-state US licensing detail is modeled for key scenarios but not exhaustive.
Jurisdictional edge-cases are rule-driven and may need legal review for production decisions.
EU and US jurisdiction coverage is baseline-generated for routing; authoritative legal text must be retrieved from foundation MCPs.
